none: Four Corners/Ambrosia/Pajariot 500 kV transmission line : environmental impact statement.

The Four Corners-Ambrosia-Pajarito 500 kV Transmission Project proposes to build a high-voltage transmission line in northwestern New Mexico. This line will stretch about 173 miles from the Four Corners Power Plant near Farmington to a new station near Albuquerque. The project aims to increase electricity transmission capacity to meet growing demand in central New Mexico. While it promises economic benefits and improved power infrastructure, it also poses environmental challenges, such as impacts on soil, vegetation, and cultural resources. Mitigation measures are planned to minimize these effects.

The Four Corners-Ambrosia-Pajarito 500 kV Transmission Project proposes to build a high-voltage transmission line in northwestern New Mexico. This line will stretch about 173 miles from the Four Corners Power Plant near Farmington to a new station near Albuquerque. The project aims to increase electricity transmission capacity to meet growing demand in central New Mexico. While it promises economic benefits and improved power infrastructure, it also poses environmental challenges, such as impacts on soil, vegetation, and cultural resources. Mitigation measures are planned to minimize these effects.
Northwestern New Mexico, from Four Corners Power Plant near Farmington to Albuquerque
Public Service Company of New Mexico

The Four Corners-Ambrosia-Pajarito 500 kV Transmission Project was proposed to address the increasing electricity demand in central New Mexico, particularly due to growth in urban areas. While the project aimed to enhance power infrastructure and provide economic benefits, it faced concerns from environmental groups and local communities regarding its potential impacts on the environment and cultural resources. Ultimately, the project has been subject to ongoing discussions about balancing energy needs with environmental protection.
